description_long: This is a large, rectangular outdoor air conditioning unit with a weathered metallic exterior, showing patches of rust and wear, especially along the lower edges and corners. The front face features a prominent circular fan protected by a wire grille, which is slightly recessed into the body. The sides display functional elements: one side has a set of horizontal louvered vents and electrical components with attached cables, while the other side is mostly plain with a couple of protruding pipes and mounting brackets. The back is flat and unadorned except for the continuation of the mounting brackets and some exposed piping. The unit stands on two sturdy metal legs, elevating it above the ground, and the overall construction suggests durability for outdoor installation in urban environments.
description: A large, weathered metal outdoor air conditioner with a circular front fan, side vents, exposed wiring, and sturdy mounting legs.
description_view_0: The front view shows a large circular fan with a wire grille, rust patches, and a flat metallic surface.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals horizontal louvered vents, a small control box, and attached cables and pipes.
description_view_2: The rear view is mostly plain metal with mounting brackets and a couple of protruding pipes visible.
description_view_3: The right side view displays a series of horizontal slats forming a vent, with mounting hardware visible at the base.
attribute_grille_pattern: The front grille is circular with concentric wire rings and radial spokes, forming a mesh pattern with moderate spacing.
attribute_control_panel_location: left side near the top, housed in a small rectangular box.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: One main exhaust vent with horizontal louvered slats is located on the right side, spanning most of the height and width of the panel.
